name: Terminator Seeds
description: Terminator seeds, also known as Genetic Use Restriction Technology (GURT), is a conspiracy theory primarily circulating within American alternative agriculture and anti-GMO movements. The core claim is that biotechnology companies intentionally engineer seeds that produce sterile plants, thus forcing farmers to repurchase seeds every growing season. This, proponents argue, creates dependence on large corporations, threatens food security, and negatively impacts biodiversity. The origins of the theory can be traced back to the late 1990s when GURT research became public. While the technology exists and patents were filed, it was never widely implemented commercially due to public opposition and ethical concerns. Proponents cite patents filed by agricultural companies, perceived corporate control over the food supply, and documented instances of unintended genetic contamination as evidence. Mainstream scientific perspectives acknowledge the existence of GURT but emphasize the limited practical application and the significant regulatory hurdles involved in its deployment. Critics argue that the theory often oversimplifies complex agricultural systems, relies on anecdotal evidence, and ignores the economic realities faced by farmers who often benefit from hybrid seeds (which naturally don't breed true) and other advanced agricultural technologies. Furthermore, they point out that seed saving is still practiced in many regions, and alternative seed sources are readily available.
subtitle: Sterile seeds and corporate control.

claims.claim: Seeds are engineered to be sterile.
claims.evidence: Patents filed for GURT technology, examples of genetic engineering.
claims.criticism: GURT was never widely implemented; most commercially available seeds are hybrids which naturally don't breed true.

claims.claim: Farmers are forced to repurchase seeds every year.
claims.evidence: Reliance on specific seed brands, perceived corporate control over agriculture.
claims.criticism: Farmers choose to purchase seeds based on yield, disease resistance, and other factors; seed saving is still practiced.

claims.evidence: Dependence on a few large corporations, potential loss of biodiversity.
claims.criticism: Modern agriculture has increased food production; regulatory oversight aims to prevent biodiversity loss; seed banks preserve genetic diversity.

keyFigures.name: Vandana Shiva
keyFigures.role: Prominent critic of GMOs and advocate against terminator seeds.

keyFigures.name: Monsanto (now Bayer)
keyFigures.role: Often cited as the primary corporation responsible, despite limited GURT implementation.

mythologyConnections: The concept of 'barren lands' and the need for renewal.
Prometheus giving fire (technology) to humanity and the consequences thereof.

timeline.event: Public outcry and protests against terminator seeds.
Terminator seed conspiracy theory continues to circulate.
